title: Section 54
content: Notwithstanding anything contained in the 1[Companies Act, 1956 (1 of 1956)], an insurance company shall not be wound up voluntarily except for the purpose of affecting an amalgamation or a re-construction of the company, or on the ground that by reason of its liabilities it cannot continue its business.
footnote: 1. Subs. by Act 11 of 2003, s. 133 and the Schedule, for "Indian Companies Act, 1913 (7 of 1913)" (w.e.f. 1-4-2003).
